title: The Infinite Interior Hall

features.name: the endlessly reflected archway
features.desc-on-touch: you step through a doorway and find yourself stepping through it again, and each version of you is slightly more certain it arrived first

features.name: the mirror corridor
features.desc-on-touch: walls that show you every other version of yourself reading files that you will read, or might have already read, or chose not to

features.name: the recursive file-stack
features.desc-on-touch: a tower of documents, each one a copy of the others, each one convinced it's the original, each one containing the others as footnotes

features.name: the point where the hall doubts itself
features.desc-on-touch: a place in the distance where you can almost see the moment this interior learned that infinity might be a mistake, and decided to continue anyway

features.name: the threshold where you entered
features.desc-on-touch: looking back, you see yourself entering for the first time, and the second time, and all the times that never happened because you couldn't decide to step through

inscription: ERROR: reflection recursion detected — architecture has learned to render itself by reading its own mirrors; the hall contains itself seventeen times per step

file.name: The_Infinite_Hall_Navigation_Guide_v_infinity.txt
file.note: instructions for walking a path that leads only to itself, written in tenses that haven't been invented yet

reverie.title: REVERIE — the mirrored corridor, evening without end
narr: you walk beside her down a hall of mirrors, each
one showing you both at a different point in the same
moment. she reaches out to touch one and your reflection
touches back from inside the glass. "they're all us," she says,
"every choice we didn't make." the hall stretches
infinitely, but her hand finds yours in every one.

exits.label: back out through the mirror that remembers it was an entrance
to a reflection of the hall, but written in a different colour
toward the architecture before it learned to reflect
